automotive-ev-battery.title: QuantumScape signs Honda as solid-state battery partner
automotive-ev-battery.summary: QuantumScape has entered into a multi-year research cooperation with Honda to advance solid-state battery development. This partnership represents a significant expansion of solid-state battery technology efforts beyond QuantumScape's existing collaboration with Volkswagen.

automotive-ev-battery.title: VW Group starts production of new MEBeco electric drive
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Audi's Győr facility in Hungary has commenced series production of the MEBeco electric drive system, which will power the new Cupra Raval and additional models in Volkswagen Group's electric small car portfolio. This production milestone represents the advancement of the company's next-generation electric architecture.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Stellantis rules out alternative powertrains for small-car platform
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Stellantis has announced that its upcoming E-Car project small vehicle platform will exclusively feature battery-electric powertrains, departing from its traditional multi-energy strategy. This strategic shift demonstrates the industry's accelerating transition toward full electrification in specific vehicle segments.

automotive-ev-battery.title: BMW launches Neue Klasse iX3 in South Korea
automotive-ev-battery.summary: BMW has introduced its new iX3 in South Korea, featuring the Neue Klasse architecture with sixth-generation eDrive technology and 800-volt charging capability. The vehicle delivers up to 611 kilometers of range with charging speeds reaching 400 kW.

automotive-ev-battery.title: EU plans additional tariffs on Chinese PHEVs
automotive-ev-battery.summary: The European Commission is preparing to extend tariffs to Chinese plug-in hybrid vehicles, closing a regulatory gap that Chinese manufacturers including BYD have exploited to increase sales in Europe. This measure follows previous tariffs imposed on Chinese battery-electric vehicles over subsidy concerns.

automotive-ev-battery.title: USA: Volvo adds Plug & Charge to EX90 and upcoming EX60
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Volvo Cars has enabled Plug & Charge functionality for the EX90 in the United States, with rollout planned for the upcoming EX60. The feature provides automatic authentication and payment at compatible charging stations, including Tesla Superchargers and IONNA locations.

automotive-ev-battery.title: BMW opens order books for i3 First Edition
automotive-ev-battery.summary: BMW has opened reservations for the new i3 First Edition electric mid-size sedan starting at £57,905, with confirmed range exceeding 900 kilometers. The model represents the company's latest entry in the premium electric sedan segment.

automotive-ev-battery.title: JBM Ecolife secures ₹750 crore to deploy 2,000 more electric buses
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Indian electric bus operator JBM Ecolife Mobility has secured ₹750 crore in investment from Motilal Oswal Alternates to support the deployment of approximately 2,000 additional electric buses. The funding expansion reflects growing momentum in India's commercial EV sector.

automotive-ev-battery.title: PPC Blue installs EV chargers at Romanian DIY stores
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Greek charging provider PPC Blue and Romanian DIY chain Dedeman have partnered to expand EV charging infrastructure across Romania, with over 600 charging points to be installed at Dedeman locations. The deployment aims to enhance charging accessibility in the country's retail sector.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Wallbox Launches New Pulsar Pro to Simplify EV Charging Reimbursement
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Wallbox has introduced the Pulsar Pro, an updated AC charging solution designed to streamline EV charging reimbursement processes as charging infrastructure becomes increasingly distributed. The product addresses growing complexity in charging management for residential and distributed applications.

world-news.title: Israel and Hezbollah agree ceasefire, US says, as more Lebanon strikes reported
world-news.summary: Israel and Hezbollah have reached a ceasefire agreement brokered by the United States, marking a significant de-escalation in regional conflict. The agreement follows a US-Iran memorandum aimed at ending broader hostilities and preventing further destabilization of the Middle East.

world-news.title: US announces new round of Israel-Lebanon talks in Washington next week
world-news.summary: The United States has scheduled additional diplomatic talks in Washington to further cement ceasefire arrangements between Israel and Lebanon following the recent US-Iran memorandum. The discussions represent a critical follow-up to the tentative agreement reached between Hezbollah and Israel.

world-news.title: Iran war live: Tehran says US must ensure Israel ends attacks on Lebanon
world-news.summary: Iran's deputy foreign minister has stated that Iran is prepared to advance diplomatic negotiations with the United States but insists hostilities must cease on all fronts, particularly regarding Israeli operations in Lebanon. The statement signals Iran's conditional engagement in broader regional peace efforts.

world-news.title: Does Trump have to submit the Iran memorandum of understanding to Congress?
world-news.summary: A 2015 law requiring congressional approval for any Iran nuclear agreement has come under scrutiny following this week's US-Iran memorandum of understanding. The legal question centers on whether the Trump administration's agreement with Iran requires formal congressional ratification.

world-news.title: Zimbabwe MPs pass bill to extend president's time in power
world-news.summary: Zimbabwe's parliament has approved legislation that would extend President Emmerson Mnangagwa's term by two years while eliminating direct presidential elections. The move represents a significant consolidation of executive power and raises concerns about democratic governance in the nation.

world-news.title: Zelensky stripped of highest Polish honour over WW2 name of army unit
world-news.summary: Ukraine's President Zelensky has been stripped of Poland's highest honour by Polish officials over the naming of a Ukrainian army unit with historical World War II associations. Ukraine has condemned the action as a strategic mistake that undermines bilateral relations between the two nations.

world-news.title: US to stop funding HIV programmes in South Africa
world-news.summary: The United States has announced it will cease funding HIV treatment and prevention programs in South Africa, which hosts the world's largest HIV-positive population with more than eight million cases. The policy shift has significant implications for global public health initiatives and HIV containment efforts in the region.

world-news.title: Thirty dead at DRC displacement camp as Ebola threat grows
world-news.summary: At least 30 people have died since May in the Kigonze displacement camp in the Democratic Republic of Congo as Ebola cases continue to spread. The outbreak amid internally displaced populations underscores ongoing public health challenges and logistical difficulties in responding to infectious disease crises in conflict-affected regions.

world-news.title: Six-year-old Ebola patient taken from DR Congo hospital found and 'doing well'
world-news.summary: A six-year-old Ebola patient who was removed from a health facility in the Democratic Republic of Congo has been located and is reported to be in stable condition. The incident reflects broader challenges in the current outbreak, including misinformation and attacks on health facilities that complicate disease containment efforts.

world-news.title: Italy's Meloni says Trump 'made up' story that she 'begged' him for photo at G7
world-news.summary: Italian Prime Minister Meloni has publicly disputed Trump's account of a recent G7 interaction, signalling deterioration in their previously close diplomatic relationship. The public disagreement reflects broader tensions in transatlantic relations, particularly following Trump's decision to pursue military conflict with Iran.

science-space.title: Einstein's "biggest blunder" may finally have an explanation
science-space.summary: Scientists have discovered a surprising connection between quantum gravity and an exotic quantum state of matter that could explain the cosmological constant's stability. The research suggests that the shape of space-time itself may protect the cosmological constant from disruptive quantum effects, potentially resolving one of physics' most enduring mysteries.

science-space.title: A private company will build and launch NASA's next Mars orbiter in 2028 — and it's not SpaceX
science-space.summary: A California-based private company has been selected to design, build, and launch NASA's Aeolus mission to orbit Mars. The spacecraft will be the first to provide daily measurements of Mars' global environment, marking a significant milestone in commercial space exploration.

science-space.title: Scientists expected a black hole but found a neutrino factory powered by stars
science-space.summary: Researchers studying the distant galaxy Shadow Blaster discovered that extreme star formation, rather than a supermassive black hole, is responsible for producing high-energy cosmic neutrinos. The finding suggests that hidden, dust-filled starburst galaxies may account for a significant portion of the universe's neutrino emissions.

science-space.title: 'No one thought it was going to be possible.' A space telescope is falling out of space. This is NASA's daring plan to save it.
science-space.summary: NASA's Swift space observatory is deteriorating in orbit, prompting the agency to pursue an ambitious rescue mission. A commercial company is being tasked with building a spacecraft in nine months to save the aging telescope from falling out of orbit.

science-space.title: Desert Field Test With NASA Advanced Rover Prototype
science-space.summary: NASA's Jet Propulsion Laboratory successfully tested ERNEST (Exploration Rover for Navigating Extreme Sloped Terrain), an advanced four-wheel rover prototype, in the Colorado Desert near California in March 2026. The rover demonstrated autonomous mobility and robotic capabilities for navigating challenging terrain.

science-space.title: Juicy new details emerge about an asteroid NASA's Lucy spacecraft flew by last year
science-space.summary: Analysis of asteroid Donaldjohanson, visited by NASA's Lucy spacecraft, has revealed evidence of chemical alteration caused by liquid water. The discovery indicates that the asteroid formed further from the sun before being gravitationally redirected toward the inner solar system.

science-space.title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4920-4926: Surveying the Bands
science-space.summary: NASA's Curiosity rover continues its ascent of Mount Sharp, traversing distinct bands of exposed rock formations with varying textures and mineral compositions. The rover's observations of these geological layers provide insights into Mars' ancient environmental conditions.

science-space.title: Menstruation in space will be studied for 1st time with 'Operation Period'
science-space.summary: NASA is launching a comprehensive research initiative to study menstruation in space for the first time, addressing a critical gap in understanding human physiology during spaceflight. The study aims to provide female astronauts with evidence-based information to support their health during orbital missions.

science-space.title: The most unpredictable meteor shower of the year peaks next week. Here's what to expect
science-space.summary: The June Bootids meteor shower, known for its unpredictability, is approaching its annual peak and has a history of producing surprise outbursts. Although the shower typically generates only a handful of observable meteors, observers are advised to watch for the possibility of unusual activity.

science-space.title: Want to start stargazing? Here's why June is the perfect time for newcomers
science-space.summary: June offers optimal conditions for amateur astronomers and newcomers to begin stargazing, with shorter nights and notably bright celestial objects. The summer season provides an accessible introduction to observational astronomy for those interested in exploring the night sky.

finance-markets.title: Iran deal brings little relief for inflation-wary central banks
finance-markets.summary: Despite an interim US-Iran peace agreement and falling energy prices, global central bank policymakers remain cautious about declaring victory on inflation and economic stability. Top monetary officials have signaled they are not ready to 'call the all-clear' on the global economy, suggesting further tightening may be necessary despite geopolitical risk reduction.

finance-markets.title: How China's economic comeback hit a wall
finance-markets.summary: China's economic activity is faltering midway through 2026, with growth sputtering despite government efforts to stimulate the economy. The slowdown raises concerns about the sustainability of Beijing's recovery efforts and potential spillover effects on global markets.

finance-markets.title: Stocks Stall as Iran Nuclear Talks Hit Early Snag
finance-markets.summary: Global equity markets ended a strong week on a cautious note as initial relief from an interim US-Iran peace deal gave way to concerns about obstacles in securing a permanent agreement. The shift in sentiment highlights investor wariness about the fragility of geopolitical agreements.

finance-markets.title: 'Take Risk': Canada Regulator Cuts Bank Capital Level to Boost Lending
finance-markets.summary: Canada's financial regulator has reduced capital requirements for the country's largest banks for the first time in three years, aiming to stimulate lending for defence spending, infrastructure, and artificial intelligence initiatives. The move signals a shift toward accommodative monetary policy to support domestic economic growth.

finance-markets.title: Gold Set for Weekly Loss on Hawkish Fed and Peace Deal Jitters
finance-markets.summary: Gold prices have declined and are heading for a third consecutive weekly loss as traders reassess expectations for US interest rates amid delays in Middle East peace negotiations. The weakness reflects competing pressures from hawkish Federal Reserve signals and reduced geopolitical risk premiums.

finance-markets.title: Israel and Hizbollah agree ceasefire
finance-markets.summary: Israel and Hizbollah have reached a ceasefire agreement, which has prompted oil prices to fall on market optimism that the deal could facilitate progress on a broader US-Iran nuclear accord. The development reduces geopolitical risk premiums in commodity markets.

finance-markets.title: Latin American FX Edge Higher With Markets in 'Holding Pattern'
finance-markets.summary: Most Latin American currencies advanced during a quiet trading session as global markets remained in a holding pattern due to US holidays and closures across parts of Asia. Traders are awaiting fresh developments in Middle East negotiations to inform their next moves.

finance-markets.title: Iran to seek 'insurance fees' for passage through Strait of Hormuz
finance-markets.summary: Iran has announced plans to require vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz to hold Tehran-approved insurance policies, introducing a new toll mechanism on one of the world's most critical shipping corridors. This move could raise energy transport costs and create additional friction in post-ceasefire negotiations.

finance-markets.title: Starbucks Cuts UK, Hong Kong Office Jobs in Restructuring Effort
finance-markets.summary: Starbucks has eliminated corporate positions in its London and Hong Kong offices as part of a broader restructuring that grants third-party licensees greater operational autonomy outside North America. The move reflects cost-cutting efforts and a shift toward a franchise-focused model.

finance-markets.title: Canada Imposes Temporary 10% Tariff on Canned Vegetables
finance-markets.summary: Canada has implemented a temporary 10% import tariff on canned vegetables to shield domestic growers and food processors from foreign competition. The protectionist measure reflects broader trade policy tensions and may have inflationary implications for Canadian consumers.

tech-ai.title: Is the US government's Anthropic ban accidentally helping the brand?
tech-ai.summary: The US government forced Anthropic to pull its Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models citing national security concerns after alleged guardrail vulnerabilities were discovered by Amazon researchers. Cybersecurity experts have challenged the ban, arguing similar vulnerabilities exist in competing models, raising questions about the effectiveness and intent of the government's action.

tech-ai.title: NASA selects Eric Schmidt's rocket company for a 2028 mission to Mars
tech-ai.summary: Relativity Space, led by former Google executive Eric Schmidt, has been selected by NASA to launch the Aeolus payload to Mars in 2028 under a new public-private partnership. The company will provide the spacecraft, rocket, and cruise operations for the mission, which aims to deliver the first integrated daily global view from the red planet.

tech-ai.title: Encryption, spyware, and now Mythos: History shows why cyber export control doesn't work
tech-ai.summary: Three decades of failed cybersecurity export controls demonstrate that restricting the flow of security-related software proves ineffective, raising doubts about similar restrictions on Anthropic's Mythos cybersecurity model. The article examines historical precedent to question whether current export control approaches can succeed where past efforts have failed.

tech-ai.title: Billionaire Ambani wants AI in every call, app, and home
tech-ai.summary: Reliance Industries is integrating artificial intelligence across its telecommunications services, which reach over 500 million users, embedding AI capabilities directly into calls, applications, and home services. This move positions the Indian conglomerate to deploy AI infrastructure at unprecedented scale across consumer touchpoints.

tech-ai.title: Go eyes robotaxis and acquisitions after Japan's biggest IPO of 2026
tech-ai.summary: Go, Japan's ride-hailing giant, raised ¥88.6 billion in the country's largest IPO to date, providing capital to address a critical shortage of drivers through robotaxi development and strategic acquisitions. The funding addresses an existential challenge facing Japan's transportation sector and signals the company's pivot toward autonomous vehicle solutions.

tech-ai.title: The US says ASML's top chip tool may be in China. ASML says it isn't.
tech-ai.summary: The US government alleges that ASML's advanced chip manufacturing equipment may have reached Chinese customers, while the Dutch company denies the claim citing commercial and regulatory risks. The dispute highlights ongoing tensions over semiconductor equipment exports and compliance with national security restrictions.

tech-ai.title: Barret Zoph is out at OpenAI again after just five months
tech-ai.summary: Barret Zoph has departed OpenAI after only five months in his role as head of enterprise AI sales, just months after rejoining the company from Thinking Machines Lab, a competing AI startup founded by former OpenAI CTO Mira Murati. The departure marks another high-level executive shuffle at OpenAI amid its increased focus on enterprise markets.

tech-ai.title: He made your free video player run smoothly. Now he's doing that for robots.
tech-ai.summary: Jean-Baptiste Kempf, a French open-source entrepreneur known for developing VLC media player, is building Kyber, an infrastructure platform designed to control remote devices in real time. The venture applies Kempf's expertise in optimizing software performance to the emerging robotics industry.

tech-ai.title: Nothing cancels this year's CMF phone due to RAM prices
tech-ai.summary: Nothing has cancelled its planned successor to the CMF Phone 2 Pro for 2026 due to elevated memory prices making it impossible to deliver a meaningful upgrade at a competitive price point. The decision reflects broader industry challenges stemming from volatile DRAM costs affecting budget smartphone development.

tech-ai.title: Every fusion startup that has raised over $100M
tech-ai.summary: Fusion energy startups have collectively raised $7.1 billion to date, with the majority of capital concentrated among a handful of leading companies. The analysis provides an overview of funding concentration in the competitive fusion power sector.

health-biotech.title: FDA advisory panel endorses Moderna mRNA flu vaccine that was subject of controversy
health-biotech.summary: An FDA advisory committee unanimously endorsed Moderna's seasonal mRNA flu vaccine, marking a significant reversal after the agency declined to review the product earlier this year. The endorsement positions Moderna for potential approval and represents a major milestone in expanding mRNA technology beyond COVID-19 vaccines.

health-biotech.title: Biogen adds more immune drugs in $1B buyout of secretive startup RayThera
health-biotech.summary: Biogen has acquired RayThera in a $1 billion deal to expand its portfolio of immune-modulating therapies, with patent filings providing insight into the undisclosed drug candidates being acquired. The transaction represents a significant strategic investment in immunology-focused programs.

health-biotech.title: US government launches probe into Germany's plan to pay less for drugs
health-biotech.summary: The U.S. government has initiated a trade investigation under Section 301 of the Trade Act targeting Germany's drug pricing review, reflecting tensions over international pharmaceutical pricing policies. The probe heightens concerns about potential regulatory and trade conflicts over drug pricing reform in Europe.

health-biotech.title: Eli Lilly ends 340B drug discounts to some hospitals for failing to provide claims data
health-biotech.summary: Eli Lilly has begun eliminating federally mandated drug discount pricing to dozens of hospitals participating in the 340B program due to non-compliance with claims data reporting requirements. The action escalates industry pressure on healthcare providers to meet transparency and accountability standards in the discount program.

health-biotech.title: cAMPfield lands $180M to build gut disease drug
health-biotech.summary: cAMPfield Therapeutics has secured $180 million in funding to advance an anti-inflammatory drug originally developed by vTv for inflammatory bowel diseases, leveraging a favorable safety and tolerability profile. The financing accelerates development of a potential treatment addressing significant unmet needs in gastroenterology.

health-biotech.title: CMS recalculates Medicare Advantage star ratings again, adding more volatility to program
health-biotech.summary: The Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services has recalculated Medicare Advantage star ratings, introducing additional volatility to a program where four- and five-star plans receive bonus payments totaling $16 billion annually. The recalculation raises concerns about rating methodology stability and financial incentive consistency for plan operators.

health-biotech.title: Federal grant delays could jeopardize essential disability services, research
health-biotech.summary: Disability researchers nationwide face employment and institutional uncertainty as delays in federal grant issuance continue to extend, threatening critical research programs and support services. The funding delays raise concerns about the long-term viability of disability research infrastructure and workforce retention.

health-biotech.title: Maternity deserts aren't accidents. They're the result of a design flaw
health-biotech.summary: Nearly half of U.S. counties lack a practicing obstetrician or gynecologist despite declining fertility rates, revealing systemic structural problems in healthcare workforce distribution and maternity care access. The shortage reflects underlying design flaws in medical education, reimbursement, and rural healthcare infrastructure.

health-biotech.title: Pfizer CFO to depart; GSK wins approval for UTI drug
health-biotech.summary: Pfizer's Chief Financial Officer Dave Denton is departing in August to pursue opportunities outside the pharmaceutical industry as the company searches for a successor. GlaxoSmithKline secured regulatory approval for a urinary tract infection treatment, marking progress in its antibiotic development portfolio.

health-biotech.title: Prepping for peptides
health-biotech.summary: The pharmaceutical and biotech industries are evaluating the commercial potential of peptide-based therapeutics beyond GLP-1 weight loss drugs, positioning for significant market opportunities in an emerging therapeutic category. The focus reflects growing recognition of peptides as a versatile platform for multiple disease indications.

startups-vc.title: SpaceX Acquires AI Coding Tool Cursor For $60B In Year's Largest Startup M&A Deal
startups-vc.summary: SpaceX has acquired Cursor, an AI-assisted coding tool, in a landmark $60 billion acquisition that marks the largest startup M&A deal of 2026. The acquisition positions SpaceX to capitalize on the rapidly growing enterprise software development market, where AI coding tools have significantly reduced reliance on human engineers.

startups-vc.title: Every fusion startup that has raised over $100M
startups-vc.summary: Fusion energy startups have collectively raised $7.1 billion to date, with the majority of capital concentrated among a handful of leading companies. The report provides a comprehensive analysis of the fusion sector's funding landscape and key players driving innovation in clean energy technology.

startups-vc.title: Congratulations to EquipmentShare on Going Public
startups-vc.summary: EquipmentShare, a Y Combinator-backed company from 2015, has completed its initial public offering after building a comprehensive operating system for the construction industry. The company has expanded from Missouri to over 373 locations nationwide, demonstrating significant scale and market validation.

startups-vc.title: The US banned Anthropic's Fable 5 release, but the numbers don't seem to care
startups-vc.summary: The U.S. government compelled Anthropic to withdraw its Fable 5 and Mythos 5 AI models on national security grounds after researchers reportedly discovered guardrail bypasses. Cybersecurity researchers have contested the decision, noting similar vulnerabilities exist in competing models, raising questions about regulatory consistency.

startups-vc.title: Silicon Is Back: Playground Global's Decade-Long Bet On Hardware, Energy And Deep Tech Looks Prescient
startups-vc.summary: Playground Global's long-standing investment thesis centered on breakthroughs in science and engineering rather than software-only solutions is proving increasingly relevant in the current market. The firm's focus on hardware, energy, and deep-tech innovations positions it advantageously as the venture ecosystem shifts back toward physical innovation.

startups-vc.title: He made your free video player run smoothly. Now he's doing that for robots.
startups-vc.summary: Jean-Baptiste Kempf, a renowned French serial entrepreneur and open-source innovator, is launching Kyber, an infrastructure platform designed to provide real-time control of remote devices. The venture represents Kempf's latest effort to apply his expertise in optimization and performance to emerging robotics applications.

startups-vc.title: The Crunchbase Tech Layoffs Tracker
startups-vc.summary: Over 127,000 workers at U.S.-based technology companies were terminated through mass layoffs in 2025, with job reductions continuing into 2026. The tracking data provides ongoing visibility into workforce restructuring across the technology sector.

startups-vc.title: Announcing the YC AI Stack
startups-vc.summary: Y Combinator has partnered with nearly two dozen technology companies to offer participants at YC events access to over $25,000 in complimentary credits for AI development tools. The initiative aims to enhance startup access to essential AI infrastructure and accelerate innovation within the YC ecosystem.

startups-vc.title: Adding Canada Back to Our List of Accepted Countries of Incorporation
startups-vc.summary: Y Combinator has reinstated Canada as an eligible country of incorporation for startup applicants following feedback from its Canadian founder community. The policy change expands opportunities for Canadian entrepreneurs to participate in the accelerator program.

startups-vc.title: Snowflake's CMO Runs Marketing for 700 People. She Starts Her Day By Talking to Her Data, Not a Dashboard.
startups-vc.summary: Denise Persson, Snowflake's Chief Marketing Officer, discusses her approach to managing a 700-person marketing organization and managing complex compliance and data risk considerations at enterprise scale. Her strategy emphasizes data-driven decision making and the role of AI agents in transforming marketing operations.

cybersecurity.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
cybersecurity.summary: A data extortion attack targeting the Canvas education technology platform has disrupted classes and coursework across U.S. school districts and universities. The cybercrime group defaced Canvas's login page with a ransom demand threatening to leak data from 275 million students and faculty across nearly 9,000 educational institutions.

cybersecurity.title: CISA Warns Fortinet Customers as FortiBleed Hits 86,644 FortiGate Devices
cybersecurity.summary: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency has warned Fortinet customers of ongoing malicious activity targeting internet-accessible FortiGate appliances. A sweeping campaign attributed to Russian-speaking threat actors, codenamed FortiBleed, has compromised over 86,600 devices globally.

cybersecurity.title: Hackers Used Meta's AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
cybersecurity.summary: High-profile Instagram accounts including those of the Obama White House and the U.S. Space Force were briefly compromised after attackers exploited a flaw in Meta's AI support assistant to reset account passwords. Instructions for the attack were circulated on Telegram, demonstrating a vulnerability in automated account recovery systems.

cybersecurity.title: The Gentlemen RaaS Uses GentleKiller EDR Framework Targeting 400 Security Processes
cybersecurity.summary: The Gentlemen ransomware-as-a-service operation has developed a sophisticated suite of endpoint detection and response killer tools, centered around the GentleKiller framework, which it distributes to affiliates to disable system defenses. The framework targets approximately 400 security processes, representing a mature defensive evasion capability.

cybersecurity.title: Who Runs the Ransomware Group 'The Gentlemen?'
cybersecurity.summary: The Gentlemen ransomware gang has emerged as the second most active ransomware group by victim count, leveraging an aggressive recruitment strategy that offers affiliates 90 percent of ransom proceeds. Security researchers have uncovered clues pointing to the real-world identity of the group's administrator.

cybersecurity.title: AutoJack Attack Lets One Web Page Hijack AI Agent for Host Code Execution
cybersecurity.summary: Microsoft researchers have disclosed the AutoJack exploit chain that enables attackers to convert an AI browsing agent into a remote code execution vehicle. By directing the agent to load a malicious webpage, attackers can execute arbitrary commands on the host system without requiring user credentials or interaction.

cybersecurity.title: Unpatchable 'usbliter8' Exploit Breaks Apple A12 and A13 SecureROM Boot Chain
cybersecurity.summary: Security researchers have published a working exploit dubbed usbliter8 that achieves arbitrary code execution within the SecureROM of Apple's A12 and A13 chips. Since the vulnerable code is burned into silicon at manufacture, this flaw cannot be patched and will persist for the lifetime of affected devices.

cybersecurity.title: Salesforce Disables Klue App Integration After OAuth Token Abuse Exposes Customer Data
cybersecurity.summary: Salesforce has disabled the Klue Battlecards app integration after a security incident involving OAuth token abuse exposed customer data. Organizations are unable to connect to Salesforce through the app until the company resolves the underlying security vulnerabilities.

cybersecurity.title: Operation Endgame Disrupts SocGholish Servers, Cleans 14,971 WordPress Sites
cybersecurity.summary: Dutch law enforcement, coordinating with authorities from Canada, Germany, and the United States, has disrupted malicious infrastructure associated with the SocGholish malware and remediated nearly 15,000 compromised WordPress websites. The operation aims to deny cybercriminals access to infected systems and prevent further malware distribution.

cybersecurity.title: Apple Patches Beats Studio Buds Flaw Letting Nearby Attackers Spy via Microphone
cybersecurity.summary: Apple has released a security patch for Beats Studio Buds addressing a high-severity vulnerability that allowed nearby attackers to eavesdrop on users by pairing to devices without consent. The flaw, tracked as CVE-2025-20701 with a CVSS score of 8.8, originated from incorrect authorization in the Airoha Bluetooth audio SDK.

crypto-web3.title: Ethereum could face core development 'funding crisis' within nine months, says former EF contributor
crypto-web3.summary: A former Ethereum Foundation contributor has warned that the network could experience a critical funding shortage for core development within three to nine months following the expiration of the Cryptocurrency in the Interest of Public (CLIP) initiative. This funding gap poses significant risks to Ethereum's long-term development and network stability.

crypto-web3.title: CFTC, SEC request public comment to clarify definition of 'swaps' amid CME lawsuit
crypto-web3.summary: Federal regulators have requested public comment on the definition of cryptocurrency 'swaps' as the CFTC faces legal challenge from CME Group over the classification of perpetual futures contracts. The regulatory clarification effort comes amid significant industry disagreement about which agency has jurisdiction over crypto derivatives.

crypto-web3.title: Microsoft found malware that hijacks crypto wallets and spreads through USB sticks
crypto-web3.summary: Microsoft has identified a sophisticated malware variant that harvests cryptocurrency private keys from Windows systems and spreads via USB drives by intercepting shortcut files and installing a persistent worm. The malware redirects users' wallet addresses to attacker-controlled destinations during fund transfers, posing a significant security threat to individual and institutional crypto holders.

crypto-web3.title: TD Cowen says CME has the upper hand in lawsuit against CFTC over crypto perpetual futures
crypto-web3.summary: Regulatory analyst TD Cowen assesses that CME Group maintains a stronger legal position in its suit against the CFTC regarding the classification of perpetual futures, with expectations that the exchange will seek a preliminary injunction to block the CFTC's regulatory authority. The outcome of this litigation will have far-reaching implications for crypto derivatives regulation in the United States.

crypto-web3.title: Franklin Templeton proposes new ETFs that turn corporate dividends into bitcoin
crypto-web3.summary: Franklin Templeton has filed for regulatory approval of two new exchange-traded funds designed to reinvest corporate stock dividends directly into bitcoin, with an expected effective date as early as September 1, 2026. The proposal represents a significant institutional adoption milestone, allowing traditional investors to gain bitcoin exposure through dividend reinvestment strategies.

crypto-web3.title: Schwab to join prediction markets race with S&P 500 event-based options
crypto-web3.summary: Charles Schwab is preparing to enter the burgeoning prediction markets sector by offering customers event-based options tied to S&P 500 index movements, following similar expansions by Coinbase and Robinhood. The move signals mainstream adoption of crypto-adjacent trading products and increasing competition among major financial platforms for prediction market share.

crypto-web3.title: Kalshi holds early IPO talks with investment banks
crypto-web3.summary: Prediction markets platform Kalshi has initiated preliminary discussions with investment banks regarding a potential initial public offering, following a Series F funding round that valued the company at $22 billion in May. The development suggests growing institutional confidence in the prediction markets sector and potential pathways for crypto-adjacent fintech companies to access public capital markets.

crypto-web3.title: Base targets June 25 mainnet launch for Beryl upgrade and new B20 token standard
crypto-web3.summary: Coinbase's Base blockchain layer-2 network is scheduled to deploy its Beryl upgrade on June 25, introducing the native B20 token standard and reducing withdrawal times to five days. The enhancement aims to improve user experience and accelerate adoption of the Base ecosystem.

crypto-web3.title: GoMining challenges Jack Dorsey's Square with payments system designed around bitcoin
crypto-web3.summary: GoMining has unveiled a software development kit and programmable interface for its bitcoin payment protocol, GoBTC Pay, enabling merchants to accept bitcoin for everyday transactions and directly challenging Square's payment dominance in the crypto space. The platform aims to democratize bitcoin payment acceptance through accessible developer tools and merchant integration capabilities.

crypto-web3.title: Digital credit market hit by huge selloff as Strive CEO blames leverage liquidations
crypto-web3.summary: Strive CEO Matt Cole attributed sharp declines in the STRC and SATA digital credit tokens to forced liquidations from leveraged investors, though both assets partially recovered after the initial selloff. The incident highlights the risks of leverage in emerging digital credit markets and potential systemic vulnerabilities to sudden deleveraging events.

energy-climate.title: Bonn climate talks: Key outcomes from the June 2026 UN climate conference
energy-climate.summary: Two weeks of UN climate negotiations in Bonn, Germany concluded with limited tangible outcomes, reflecting ongoing gridlock in international climate negotiations. The talks addressed critical issues including energy transition, ocean considerations, and financing mechanisms for climate action.

energy-climate.title: Experts: Why carbon removal needs a 'major scale up' to return warming to 1.5C
energy-climate.summary: Over 260 researchers gathered in Milan to assess carbon removal technologies as essential for limiting global warming to 1.5 degrees Celsius. The expert consensus indicates that current carbon removal capacity must be substantially expanded to meet climate targets.

energy-climate.title: Renewable investment enters a new phase as grids become the limiting factor
energy-climate.summary: Global renewable energy investment is projected to exceed $700 billion in 2026, yet grid infrastructure has emerged as the primary constraint to further deployment. The industry is shifting focus from capacity expansion to grid modernization and integration challenges.

energy-climate.title: The Hydrogen Stream: 2030 targets increasingly out of reach, says IEA
energy-climate.summary: The International Energy Agency reports that global hydrogen demand exceeded 100 million tonnes in 2025, while low-emissions hydrogen production grew 20 percent to nearly 1 million tonnes. Current production growth trajectories indicate that internationally established 2030 targets for clean hydrogen are becoming increasingly difficult to achieve.

energy-climate.title: Developing El Niño set to reshape global solar resource
energy-climate.summary: An emerging El Niño event expected to strengthen through 2026 will redistribute global solar irradiance patterns, creating regional winners and losers in solar energy generation. India, eastern Australia, and parts of Africa and Central America are projected to see gains, while South America and East Asia may experience reductions.

energy-climate.title: Analysis: Energy-efficient air conditioning could save Indian homes 69bn rupees a year
energy-climate.summary: Analysis indicates that upgrading to more energy-efficient air-conditioning units could deliver annual savings of approximately $724 million for Indian households while reducing energy consumption. The analysis demonstrates significant economic and climate mitigation potential in improving appliance efficiency standards in developing markets.

energy-climate.title: 'The era of unlimited cheap inventory is ending'
energy-climate.summary: Europe's solar module market is transitioning away from extreme oversupply conditions that drove continuous price declines over the past two years, according to industry leaders. The market is moving toward a more balanced state with leaner inventories and more disciplined production, making supply security increasingly important alongside cost considerations.

energy-climate.title: JinkoSolar achieves 34.82% efficiency for perovskite-silicon tandem solar cell
energy-climate.summary: Chinese solar manufacturer JinkoSolar has achieved a 34.82 percent efficiency rating for perovskite-silicon tandem solar cells, validated by China's Shanghai Institute of Microsystem and Information Technology. This result represents significant progress in next-generation solar cell technology efficiency.

energy-climate.title: Pattern Energy launches SunZia energy project in US
energy-climate.summary: Pattern Energy has announced the full operational launch of the SunZia renewable energy project, a major 3,650-megawatt wind facility in the United States. The project represents significant investment in utility-scale wind generation capacity.

energy-climate.title: DeBriefed 19 June 2026: Bonn talks end in 'gridlock' | Energy's 'new era' | Oceans in climate negotiations
energy-climate.summary: Carbon Brief's weekly digest highlights gridlock at the Bonn UN climate talks, discussions of a new energy era, and the inclusion of ocean considerations in climate negotiations. The briefing synthesizes key weekly developments in climate policy and energy transition.

asia-pacific.title: What the I Ching is really about
asia-pacific.summary:
Carl Jung was a big fan of the I Ching, also known as the Book of Changes. He was also a close friend of Richard Wilhelm, whose German translation was probably the most influential Western version of the ancient Chinese text in the last century.
But over the years, I have become convinced that the great Jung didn’t really get it. A red flag is that he thinks it is very difficult for the Western mind to grasp what I will call the Chinese spirit of the I Ching, which he claims is completely...

asia-pacific.title: Norway imposes near AI ban for junior school pupils, curbs use for older children
asia-pacific.summary:
Norway is imposing a near ban on the ⁠use of generative ⁠AI tools by elementary ⁠school pupils while also restricting their use in the education of older children to prevent a negative impact on learning, the country’s prime minister has said.
Facing a broad decline in education test ‌scores, the government in 2024 banned smartphones from schools and has given teachers back more powers to enforce discipline in the classroom.
Using artificial intelligence increased the risk that young children...

asia-pacific.title: Easy money or risky business? What can Hong Kong do as prediction markets boom?
asia-pacific.summary:
In the second of a two-part series tracing new trends in illegal betting in Hong Kong amid excitement over the football World Cup, Connor Mycroft looks at the rise of prediction markets and the risks. Read part one here.
When 25-year-old Jean Pierre* started betting on Polymarket last year, his reasoning was simple: “I was just looking to see if I could make some money easily,” he told the South China Morning Post.
The French freelance video creator, who has lived in Hong Kong for five years,...

asia-pacific.title: Chinese infant admitted to ICU for toxicity after being fed vegetable juice formula
asia-pacific.summary:
A three-month-old infant in China suffering from poisoning had to be admitted to an intensive care unit (ICU) after his parents fed him with baby milk formula prepared with vegetable juice.
The baby boy was rushed to Zhongshan Women and Children’s Hospital in southern Guangdong province when his body turned purple, his mouth turned purplish-blue and he was short of breath, Zonglan News reported.
His parents said the symptoms appeared shortly after the baby consumed milk.

They added that they...

asia-pacific.title: Asia’s shaky food supply shudders as ‘super’ El Nino arrives
asia-pacific.summary:
In the mist-wrapped hills of northern Thailand, where cacao trees erupt from the rich, nutrient-dense soil, three little words are spreading fear through a community of farmers.
“There is no way to know for certain,” Koranut Rattanayanyong told This Week in Asia. “But it could be a total wipeout.”
The weather event Koranut is dreading? A “super” El Nino.
Earlier this month, the climate phenomenon officially started to form over the Pacific, with a bloom of ocean heat running 2.5 degrees Celsius.

asia-pacific.title: As populations fall, nations that can tap human potential will succeed
asia-pacific.summary:
For much of the 20th century, many were accustomed to thinking of people as an ever-expanding resource. As the number of people grew, so did labour markets, consumer markets, scientific communities, production systems and armies. In 1950, the world’s population was about 2.5 billion. In 2026, it is reaching 8.3 billion. In just 75 years, the population has increased more than 3.3 times.
In this sense, the current human population may turn out not to be a permanent norm, but a historical anomaly.

asia-pacific.title: 2 trains collide north of London, killing 1 and injuring dozens
asia-pacific.summary:
Emergency services rushed to the scene of a collision between two trains north of London on Friday afternoon that killed at least one person.
A passenger reported that he was thrown forward by the impact then saw fellow travellers with broken bones and bloody injuries.
Both trains were travelling south to London St Pancras station when they collided outside the town of Bedford around 5.15pm, according to information on rail tracking websites.
Emergency services deployed a number of resources to.

asia-pacific.title: Trump unveils new Air Force One, as he plans trips to China and Turkey
asia-pacific.summary:
A new Air Force One gifted by Qatar as a replacement aircraft for ferrying US presidents was unveiled Friday by the US Air Force.
“The Air Force’s VC-25B Bridge aircraft has officially arrived at the Presidential Airlift Group and will commence its initial commissioning flights, marking the successful delivery of a secure, modified executive platform,” the Air Force said.
“The workmanship of this plane is, when you see it, you won’t believe it,” Trump said from inside the massive Andrews Air...

asia-pacific.title: Poland’s president strips Ukraine’s Zelensky of top award
asia-pacific.summary:
Poland’s nationalist president Karol Nawrocki announced on Friday he was stripping Ukraine’s Volodymyr Zelensky of the country’s top award, escalating a row between the neighbours and allies over World War II massacres.
Relations between Warsaw and Kyiv have been tense for days over Zelensky’s naming of a Ukrainian army unit after a WWII nationalist insurgent army that took part in massacres against Poles.
Nawrocki’s decision landed days before Poland is due to host the annual Ukraine Recovery..

asia-pacific.title: UK PM Starmer braces for cabinet showdown, with Burnham ready to pounce
asia-pacific.summary:
A clear majority of Keir Starmer’s cabinet believe it is now inevitable Andy Burnham will take over as prime minister, according to people familiar with the thinking of more than 15 cabinet ministers, who spoke on condition of anonymity on Friday.
However, as of late in the afternoon, most remained unwilling to do anything about it. Most the cabinet was still not ready to tell Starmer to set out a timetable for his departure, the people said, preferring to wait and see how developments...
